text.title: C# Clean Architecture + CQRS Failure Modes
text.summary: 7 antipatterns observed in real Clean Architecture deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Domain entities become property bags; every business rule sits in `UserService` handlers. Code review shows zero non-getter methods on `User`.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent adds new behaviour to Application handler instead of the Domain entity.
antipattern.fix: Move the rule onto an entity method (`user.Activate()`). Prompt agents to open `Domain/Entities/<Aggregate>.cs` first when changing behaviour.

antipattern.symptom: Application project compiles with `using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;`. NetArchTest run is missing or skipped in CI.
antipattern.root-cause: Author imported EF into Application "for convenience" and reviewer let it land.
antipattern.fix: Enable a NetArchTest fitness assertion: `Types.InAssembly(Application).Should().NotHaveDependencyOn("Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore")`. Fail the build.

antipattern.symptom: Cross-aggregate handler triggered inside `SaveChangesAsync`; downstream failure rolls back the originating write unexpectedly.
antipattern.root-cause: Domain events dispatched inline instead of via outbox.
antipattern.fix: Persist domain events to an outbox table inside the same transaction; dispatch asynchronously via MassTransit Outbox or a hosted relay.

antipattern.symptom: `GET /health` wrapped in `GetHealthQuery`/`GetHealthQueryHandler`; profile shows handler resolution dominating request latency.
antipattern.root-cause: Every endpoint dispatched through MediatR by default.
antipattern.fix: Reserve MediatR for use-cases that benefit from the pipeline (validation, auth, transaction). Trivial reads stay as plain controller actions.

antipattern.symptom: Handler does `user.Posts.Add(post)`; invariants bypassed; domain events not raised.
antipattern.root-cause: Collection exposed as mutable `ICollection<T>` and mutated directly.
antipattern.fix: Expose collections as `IReadOnlyCollection<T>`; mutate via aggregate methods (`user.AddPost(post)`). Raise the domain event inside the aggregate.

antipattern.symptom: Multi-tenant query returns rows from other tenants in production; audit log shows `IgnoreQueryFilters()` in a non-admin repository.

root-cause.source: EF Core docs — global query filters

antipattern.root-cause: Agent reached for `IgnoreQueryFilters()` to "see all rows" and shipped it.
antipattern.fix: Forbid `IgnoreQueryFilters()` outside `Infrastructure/Admin/` via a Roslyn analyzer rule; require code review approval for any new use.

antipattern.symptom: `INotificationHandler<T>` declared `async void`; one unhandled exception inside the handler tears down the host.
antipattern.fix: Return `Task`. The Notification dispatch already runs in the background — no `void` required. Add Roslyn analyzer `VSTHRD100`.
